merged_findings: Average Packet Size consistently exhibits high class-conditioned distribution separation with low cardinality, confirming structural variation linked to class.
Bwd Packet Length Max and Mean features show stable, near-constant or significantly imbalanced variance patterns aligned with DDoS signatures, reinforcing class-conditioned structural variation.
No dataset-level duplication bias detected, though redundancy between Bwd Packet Length Mean and Avg Bwd Segment Size indicates overlapping feature information.
Local label-topology conflicts and moderate label neighborhood conflicts persist in some features, preserving epistemic tension and limiting conclusive stability assessment.
Average Packet Size provides moderate shortcut-like predictive signal but does not alone confirm shortcut exploitation.
open_gaps: local label-topology conflicts limit conclusive stability assessment
analysis limited to bounded feature pairs and dataset-level duplication without deeper metadata linkage
update_focus: consolidate class-conditioned structural variation evidence while preserving local label-topology conflicts and redundancy limitations
